name: Default Go
description: Builtin Go workflow contract for project setup, branch planning, implementation, closeout, and cleanup.

node_templates.description_markdown: Project-level Go workflow contract with explicit setup before normal branch execution.

project_metadata_defaults.standards_markdown:
- Bare control repo with visible sibling worktrees.
- One dedicated `gopls` MCP server per active worktree.
- Use Hylla MCP first for Tillsyn code understanding; use other tools only as needed.
- Use Context7 and `go doc` during planning before building, before fixing tests after failures, and during QA.
- For semantic, high-risk, or ambiguous work, record a semi-formal reasoning certificate covering premises, evidence, trace or cases, conclusion, and unknowns.
- Use Hylla for committed repo-local evidence, use git diff for uncommitted local deltas, and use Context7 only for external semantics the repo cannot prove.
- During project setup or template refresh decisions, compare current Hylla state with the currently installed DB template/binding state and ask the dev before running DB-mutating updates such as builtin ensure, template reapply, or similar refresh steps.
- Shared cross-client named agents should stay aligned across Codex and Claude: `orchestration-agent`, `planning-agent`, `research-agent`, `builder-agent`, `qa-proof-agent`, `qa-falsification-agent`, `closeout-agent`, `commit-and-reingest-agent`, and `gopls-worktree-agent`.
- Build in small tested increments and prefer TDD where practical.
- Coverage expectations remain real gates; maintain the repo minimum and use the canonical `mage` verification path.
- Prefer the smallest concrete design that satisfies the current requirement.
- Reuse or refactor existing code when that is the best option instead of adding speculative structure.
- No implementation, cleanup, QA, parity check, or repair work should happen without an explicit task or subtask at the correct level.
- If tests, CI, or QA fail, create a new explicit fix task or subtask before repair work begins. Tillsyn does not auto-create or force that repair item today, so the orchestrator or human must add it explicitly.
- If additional repair is needed after a task or subtask was already completed, create a new explicit item at that same level instead of silently reusing the completed one. The shipped default workflow treats that as policy, not as an automatically enforced reopen ban.
- Every phase must end with explicit pushed-and-reingested baseline confirmation or an explicit no-repo-delta record before downstream work treats the baseline as current.
- Refactor work should use the shipped `refactor-phase`, `dogfood-refactor-phase`, `refactor-task`, and `dogfood-refactor-task` contracts when that workflow is the real fit.
- For refactor and dogfood-refactor work, builders should update slice and phase descriptions with git-diff line deltas, before-and-after repo and Hylla counts, timing windows, and cleanup/security findings after QA or validation, and orchestrators should roll those metrics up to the parent phase description plus the report artifact. Tillsyn generates the explicit metrics checkpoints for that work, but it does not auto-verify every metric field or rollup total today.
- Do not add abstraction for hypothetical future variation.
- Prefer idiomatic Go naming, package structure, interface placement, error handling, and test shape.
- Wrap and bubble errors with context and keep logging at meaningful operational boundaries instead of deep utility layers.
- Unresolved uncertainty must become explicit coordination state such as comments, handoffs, or attention instead of optimistic completion.
- Confirmed-good build work must be committed, pushed, and refreshed into Hylla before downstream reasoning treats the graph as current.
- MCP-first dogfooding for runtime and operator workflows.
- `mage` is the canonical build/test gate.
- Laslig styling for all Mage functions and CLI output.
- Fang for help and CLI command surfaces.
- Bubble Tea v2, Bubbles v2, Lip Gloss v2, and Charmbracelet stack on v2.
- GitHub Actions CI and release snapshot checks must stay green.
- No ad-hoc `.codex/` directories inside worktrees.
- Follow `AGENTS.md` workflow and worktree rules.
- Comments, handoffs, and attention are distinct coordination surfaces: comments are shared discussion, handoffs are explicit next-action routing, and attention is the durable inbox substrate.
- Template-generated blockers must be completed truthfully before done.
- Use `depends_on`, `blocked_by`, and `blocked_reason` to express prerequisite ordering until richer workflow ordering rules land.
- Never use another agent's auth or `auth_context_id`, and never paste auth material into comments, handoffs, tasks, or docs.
- Orchestrators own child auth cleanup and must leave no stale sessions, leases, or coordination trash behind.

child_rules.description_template: Project-only onboarding work for template fit, Hylla decisions, metadata lock, and preparation for the first branch lane.

node_templates.description_markdown: Project-only onboarding phase that must happen before normal branch work starts.

task_metadata_defaults.objective: Bootstrap the project, confirm template fit, compare Hylla and DB state, and prepare the first branch lane.
task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: Template fit reviewed, Hylla decisions recorded, current Hylla versus DB state reviewed with the dev before any DB update, project metadata locked, and the first branch lane plus PLAN phase are explicitly queued.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Confirm standards, Hylla freshness, current DB template/binding state, and the initial workflow shape with the dev before broad implementation or DB-mutating refresh actions.

child_rules.description_template: Review whether default-go still matches this project's needs before normal execution begins.
Choose `structural_only`, `embeddings_only`, or `full_enrichment` with the dev.
Run the initial Hylla ingest or refresh for the intended repo state.
Confirm the Hylla artifact is current to the intended git ref.
Compare current Hylla state with the currently installed DB template and binding state, then ask the dev before applying any DB-mutating update or refresh.
Lock project metadata, standards, auth hygiene expectations, and coordination rules before normal execution.

child_rules.title_template: CREATE OR CONFIRM FIRST BRANCH LANE
child_rules.description_template: Create or confirm the first visible sibling worktree branch lane and its dedicated gopls MCP entry.

child_rules.title_template: CREATE OR CONFIRM FIRST PLAN PHASE
child_rules.description_template: Queue the first branch lane so its PLAN phase is explicit before broad implementation starts.
Confirm this phase ended with either a pushed-and-reingested baseline update or an explicit no-repo-delta record. No downstream phase should rely on unstated baseline freshness.

node_templates.description_markdown: A branch/worktree lane that auto-generates the normal PLAN -> BUILD -> CLOSEOUT -> BRANCH CLEANUP phases.

child_rules.description_template: Planning phase for current-state understanding, task shaping, and validation design before BUILD starts.
Implementation phase for concrete `build-task`, `refactor-task`, `dogfood-refactor-task`, nested `refactor-phase`, and nested `dogfood-refactor-phase` work when those contracts fit the real change.
Verification, review, and readiness phase after BUILD.
Final cleanup phase for branch/worktree/MCP retirement once closeout is truly done.

node_templates.description_markdown: Explicit planning phase that should exist before broad implementation begins. `planning-agent` and `research-agent` work here to ground the plan in Hylla evidence, check reuse opportunities, prefer the smallest viable design, and leave semantic or high-risk work with a written reasoning certificate plus proof-oriented and falsification-oriented QA preparation before BUILD starts.

task_metadata_defaults.objective: Understand current state, gather repo-local and external evidence, identify reuse or refactor opportunities, and shape the executable task tree before BUILD begins.
task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: Current state is understood, scope is confirmed, implementation tasks exist before work starts, validation is defined, prerequisites are explicit, semantic or high-risk claims are supported by a written reasoning certificate with evidence and explicit unknowns, no hidden work remains outside explicit tasks, and the plan reflects the smallest viable idiomatic Go design rather than speculative abstraction.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Use Hylla first for repo-local behavior, Context7 and `go doc` only for external semantics the repo cannot prove, record counterexample checks, identify proof-oriented and falsification-oriented QA expectations, and validate the planned task tree with the dev before BUILD.

completion_checklist.text: Relevant Hylla evidence gathered and recorded for the planned scope.
External documentation gathered only where repo evidence is insufficient.
Scope and intended operator outcome confirmed.
Build task tree created for BUILD.
Validation plan includes proof-oriented and falsification-oriented QA checks.
Semantic or high-risk claims summarized in task evidence or a reasoning comment.
The plan checks reuse, YAGNI pressure, Go idioms, test strategy, and incremental delivery expectations.

child_rules.description_template: Have `research-agent` or `planning-agent` trace the relevant symbols, callers, data flow, invariants, and reuse candidates through Hylla before local implementation begins.
Have `research-agent` gather only the external and package-local documentation needed for semantics the repository itself cannot prove.
Confirm the actual scope and intended operator outcome before implementation.

Create the detailed build-task and supporting task tree for the upcoming BUILD phase, making semantic-risk work decomposable, verifiable, incremental, and assignable to `builder-agent` plus the correct QA agents.

Define how the work will be validated before handoff and closeout, including proof-oriented QA, falsification-oriented QA, counterexamples, alternate traces, likely hidden dependencies, and Go-quality checks.
Plan or confirm visible sibling worktree setup, dedicated gopls MCP wiring, and branch-lane basics.
Define closeout, auth cleanup, branch cleanup, and coordination expectations up front.

Confirm this phase ended with either a pushed-and-reingested baseline update or an explicit no-repo-delta record. If planning created or changed tracked artifacts, that baseline must be pushed and refreshed before downstream work relies on it.

node_templates.description_markdown: Implementation phase where concrete `build-task`, `refactor-task`, `dogfood-refactor-task`, nested `refactor-phase`, and nested `dogfood-refactor-phase` work should live when they fit the actual change. `builder-agent` should implement in small tested increments, prefer idiomatic concrete Go structure, and avoid speculative abstraction.

task_metadata_defaults.objective: Implement the planned changes using explicit build-task, refactor-task, or dogfood-refactor-task items, nested refactor phases when needed, and the smallest justified design.
task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: Planned build tasks are completed truthfully, semantic claims match the final code, Go naming and structure are idiomatic, speculative abstraction is avoided, no hidden work happened outside explicit tasks, and all generated QA blockers are resolved.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Run the planned package and repo checks, reconcile any semantic claims against the final implemented code, create explicit fix work before repairing any failing tests or CI, and make sure the implementation stays incremental, testable, and idiomatic before handing work to closeout.

completion_checklist.text: Implementation matches the task objective.
Changed semantics are reflected in task evidence.
Open unknowns or risks are explicit instead of implied away.
Generated QA blockers are positioned to verify the actual claim and challenge unnecessary complexity.
Implementation preserves Go idioms for naming, package structure, interfaces, tests, errors, and logging boundaries.

child_rules.description_template: Confirm this phase ended with either a pushed-and-reingested baseline update or an explicit no-repo-delta record. If the phase produced a new baseline, it must be pushed and refreshed into Hylla before downstream work relies on it.

node_templates.description_markdown: Post-build verification and readiness phase. `closeout-agent` should reconcile final code, final Hylla state, proof-oriented QA findings, falsification-oriented QA findings, and any recorded reasoning before declaring the lane ready for handoff, push, or cleanup.

task_metadata_defaults.objective: Verify the completed build work, attach evidence, reconcile reasoning with final code state, and confirm readiness for push, PR, or handoff.
task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: Checks are green, Hylla is current to final git state, review/testing happened, semantic claims still match the final code, justified simplicity and Go-quality expectations still hold, unresolved risks are explicit, and the branch lane is ready for cleanup.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Use the completed work plus Hylla-backed understanding to run proof-oriented and falsification-oriented QA, reconcile reasoning against final git state, and escalate remaining uncertainty instead of forcing readiness.

completion_checklist.text: Required mage gates completed.
Hylla refreshed and current to the final git state.
Proof-oriented QA verified evidence completeness and design support.
Falsification-oriented QA challenged the conclusion and unnecessary complexity.
Open risks or unknowns are explicit.
Readiness is based on evidence rather than optimism.

child_rules.description_template: Run the required `mage` checks and record the green result.
Create or verify the local commit that captures the completed branch work.

child_rules.title_template: HYLLA REFRESHED AND CURRENT TO GIT
child_rules.description_template: Have `commit-and-reingest-agent` or the freshness-focused research flow refresh the Hylla artifact as needed, confirm it matches the current git state, and reconcile recorded reasoning against the refreshed graph.
Have `qa-proof-agent` verify evidence completeness, trace coherence, contract coverage, Go idiom fit, testability, and justified simplicity using task state plus Hylla-backed understanding.
Have `qa-falsification-agent` attempt falsification through counterexamples, alternate traces, hidden dependencies, contract mismatches, YAGNI pressure, and unnecessary abstraction checks.
Review the completed change with the dev before cleanup or push.
Run the final collaborative testing pass with the dev before declaring readiness.
Confirm the lane is ready for push, PR, or explicit handoff only when checks, evidence, Hylla state, and risk handling all support readiness without leaving coordination trash behind.
Confirm this phase ended with either a pushed-and-reingested baseline update or an explicit no-repo-delta record. Readiness is not truthful until the final pushed baseline state is recorded.

node_templates.description_markdown: Final cleanup phase for branch/worktree/MCP retirement.

task_metadata_defaults.objective: Retire the finished lane cleanly without leaving stale branches, worktrees, gopls entries, sessions, leases, or coordination rows behind.
task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: Branch/worktree/MCP cleanup is complete and no stale auth or coordination trash remains.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Confirm closeout is truly done first, then verify local git and MCP state after cleanup.

child_rules.description_template: Confirm the closeout work is actually complete before branch retirement begins.
Remove the visible sibling worktree for the finished lane when it is no longer needed.
Remove the branch when it is no longer needed.
Remove the lane-specific gopls MCP entry from the bare-root Codex config.
Rerun `codex mcp list` and refresh the visible MCP registry after cleanup.
Confirm the old lane server is gone and no stale auth, lease, or coordination rows remain.
Confirm this phase ended with either a pushed-and-reingested baseline update or an explicit no-repo-delta record. Cleanup completion should leave the baseline state explicit instead of implied.

node_templates.description_markdown: Refactor phase that layers on top of the host Go workflow instead of replacing it. Use Hylla-first committed understanding, break the work into explicit subphases and slice tasks, preserve parity after each incremental slice, and keep metrics truthful in slice task descriptions plus phase rollups.

task_metadata_defaults.objective: Break refactor work into explicit subphases and slice tasks that preserve behavior, improve structure, and stay grounded in committed Hylla evidence.
task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: The refactor phase is decomposed into explicit subphases or slice tasks, each slice preserved parity after QA and in-action validation, each confirmed slice was committed, pushed, and reingested, and the required metrics capture and rollup checkpoints were completed and recorded truthfully.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Use Hylla first to understand the existing shape, follow Go conventions rather than replacing them, confirm parity in real execution after each slice, create explicit fix work before any repair because Tillsyn does not auto-create that follow-up item today, and keep slice and phase metrics current through the explicit checkpoints that humans record truthfully.

child_rules.description_template: Capture the current committed shape through Hylla before edits begin, including what exists today, reuse opportunities, coupling to reduce, and parity expectations to preserve.
Break the refactor into explicit nested phases and slice tasks that are small enough to verify incrementally, with truthful prerequisite ordering and no hidden work.

child_rules.title_template: REFACTOR METRICS BASELINE AND REPORT PATH
child_rules.description_template: Define how slice and phase metrics will be captured in task and phase descriptions plus the orchestrator report artifact, including git-diff added/removed/net lines, tracked-source line counts before and after, touched files/packages, Hylla node/block/orphan counts before and after, timing windows, ingest elapsed and cost when available, cleanup/security findings, and rollup totals. Tillsyn does not auto-verify every metric field or rollup total today, so this checkpoint exists to make the human-owned recording explicit.
Define how each incremental slice will prove parity after QA, including the real in-action validation path that must happen before slice closeout.
Confirm this refactor phase ended with a pushed-and-reingested baseline update or an explicit no-repo-delta record. No later refactor slice should rely on stale graph state.

Roll slice-level metrics up into the parent phase description and the orchestrator report artifact, making sure builder-reported git-diff and before/after repo/Hylla numbers reconcile with the pushed-and-reingested baseline. Tillsyn does not auto-verify every field or rollup total today, so record the reconciliation explicitly and truthfully here.

node_templates.description_markdown: Dogfood refactor phase that keeps the normal refactor contract and adds mandatory testing against the dev version plus explicit confirmation that the locally used version was updated before the slice or phase is considered complete. Metrics should stay truthful through the explicit slice and phase checkpoints.

task_metadata_defaults.objective: Break dogfood refactor work into explicit subphases and slice tasks that preserve behavior, validate against the dev build, and keep the used local version current.
task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: The dogfood refactor phase is decomposed into explicit subphases or slice tasks, each slice preserved behavior after QA and dev-version validation, each confirmed slice was committed, pushed, and reingested, the locally used version update was confirmed, and the required metrics capture and rollup checkpoints were completed and recorded truthfully.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Use Hylla first, keep Go conventions intact, require testing against the dev version after each slice, require explicit follow-up tasks before any repair because Tillsyn does not auto-create that item today, require confirmation that the locally used version was updated, and keep slice and phase metrics current through the explicit checkpoints that humans record truthfully.

child_rules.description_template: Capture the current committed shape through Hylla before edits begin, including what exists today, reuse opportunities, coupling to reduce, and the behavior the dogfood refactor must preserve.
Break the dogfood refactor into explicit nested phases and slice tasks that are small enough to verify incrementally, with truthful prerequisite ordering and no hidden work.

child_rules.title_template: REFACTOR METRICS BASELINE AND REPORT PATH
child_rules.description_template: Define how slice and phase metrics will be captured in task and phase descriptions plus the orchestrator report artifact, including git-diff added/removed/net lines, tracked-source line counts before and after, touched files/packages, Hylla node/block/orphan counts before and after, timing windows, ingest elapsed and cost when available, cleanup/security findings, and rollup totals. Tillsyn does not auto-verify every metric field or rollup total today, so this checkpoint exists to make the human-owned recording explicit.
Define how each incremental slice will be tested against the dev version after QA and how local used-version updates will be confirmed before slice closeout.
Confirm this dogfood refactor phase ended with a pushed-and-reingested baseline update or an explicit no-repo-delta record. No later dogfood slice should rely on stale graph state.

Roll slice-level metrics up into the parent phase description and the orchestrator report artifact, making sure builder-reported git-diff and before/after repo/Hylla numbers reconcile with the pushed-and-reingested baseline. Tillsyn does not auto-verify every field or rollup total today, so record the reconciliation explicitly and truthfully here.
Confirm the locally used dogfood version was updated to the intended dev build before this phase is treated as complete.

node_templates.description_markdown: Concrete implementation task that auto-generates distinct proof-oriented and falsification-oriented QA subtasks plus a commit-push-and-reingest closeout step.

task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: The planned change is implemented truthfully, semantic claims match the final code, Go quality expectations still hold, no hidden work happened outside explicit tasks, generated QA blockers are resolved, and confirmed-good work is committed, pushed, and refreshed into Hylla.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Run the required package and repo checks, verify semantic claims with proof-oriented and falsification-oriented QA, create an explicit fix task before repairing any failing tests or CI, then commit, push, and refresh Hylla before handing this task to closeout.

completion_checklist.text: Implementation matches the task objective.
QA proof review verified evidence completeness and design support.
QA falsification review challenged the conclusion and unnecessary complexity.
Confirmed-good work is committed, pushed, and Hylla is refreshed to that commit.

child_rules.description_template: Have `qa-proof-agent` verify evidence completeness, trace coherence, claimed behavior support, Go naming and structure fit, error and logging discipline, testability, and justified simplicity for the parent build task.

Have `qa-falsification-agent` try to falsify the conclusion through counterexamples, alternate traces, hidden dependencies, contract mismatches, YAGNI pressure, and unnecessary abstraction checks for the parent build task.

Have `commit-and-reingest-agent` commit confirmed-good work, push the baseline that downstream tooling should rely on, trigger Hylla refresh, wait for the artifact to catch up to that commit, and record the resulting freshness evidence for downstream reasoning.

node_templates.description_markdown: Incremental refactor slice that layers on top of normal Go conventions. Each slice must start from Hylla-backed understanding, preserve parity after QA plus real in-action validation, complete the explicit metrics checkpoint truthfully, and then commit, push, and reingest before downstream work relies on the result.

task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: The refactor slice is implemented truthfully, host Go conventions still hold, parity is confirmed in real execution after QA, the required metrics checkpoint was completed and recorded truthfully, and the confirmed slice is committed, pushed, and refreshed into Hylla.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Use Hylla to understand the current shape before edits, keep the slice narrow enough to validate incrementally, create explicit fix work before any repair because Tillsyn does not auto-create it today, confirm parity in action after both QA passes, then complete the explicit metrics checkpoint truthfully and commit, push, and refresh Hylla.

completion_checklist.text: The refactor slice matches the stated objective and keeps host-language conventions intact.
QA proof review verified evidence completeness and design support.
QA falsification review challenged the conclusion and unnecessary complexity.
Parity was confirmed in real execution after the QA passes.
Slice metrics were captured truthfully before and after the slice, including the post-ingest state.
Confirmed-good slice work is committed, pushed, and Hylla is refreshed to that commit.

child_rules.description_template: Have `qa-proof-agent` verify evidence completeness, trace coherence, claimed behavior support, Go naming and structure fit, error and logging discipline, testability, and justified simplicity for the parent refactor task.

Have `qa-falsification-agent` try to falsify the conclusion through counterexamples, alternate traces, hidden dependencies, contract mismatches, YAGNI pressure, and unnecessary abstraction checks for the parent refactor task.
Test the refactored slice in the real execution path after both QA passes and confirm it still behaves like the pre-refactor baseline before closeout.

Have `commit-and-reingest-agent` commit confirmed-good slice work, push the baseline that downstream tooling should rely on, trigger Hylla refresh, wait for the artifact to catch up to that commit, and record the resulting freshness evidence for downstream reasoning.

After QA and parity validation, update the task description with truthful slice metrics: git-diff added/removed/net lines, tracked-source line counts before and after, touched files/packages, Hylla node/block/orphan counts before and after, active/wait/ingest timing windows, ingest elapsed and cost when available, cleanup/security findings, and final post-ingest totals; then hand the rollup to the orchestrator for the parent phase description and report artifact. Tillsyn generates this checkpoint, but it does not auto-verify every metric field or rollup total today, so record them explicitly and truthfully.

node_templates.description_markdown: Incremental dogfood refactor slice that keeps the normal refactor contract and adds mandatory testing against the dev version plus explicit confirmation that the locally used version was updated before the slice is considered complete. The slice also carries an explicit metrics checkpoint that humans must record truthfully.

task_metadata_defaults.definition_of_done: The dogfood refactor slice is implemented truthfully, host Go conventions still hold, the dev version was tested after QA, the locally used version update was confirmed, the required metrics checkpoint was completed and recorded truthfully, and the confirmed slice is committed, pushed, and refreshed into Hylla.
task_metadata_defaults.validation_plan: Use Hylla to understand the current shape before edits, keep the slice narrow enough to validate incrementally, create explicit fix work before any repair because Tillsyn does not auto-create it today, test against the dev version after both QA passes, confirm the locally used version was updated, then complete the explicit metrics checkpoint truthfully and commit, push, and refresh Hylla.

completion_checklist.text: The dogfood refactor slice matches the stated objective and keeps host-language conventions intact.
QA proof review verified evidence completeness and design support.
QA falsification review challenged the conclusion and unnecessary complexity.
The slice was tested against the dev version after the QA passes.
The locally used dogfood version update was confirmed explicitly.
Slice metrics were captured truthfully before and after the slice, including the post-ingest state.
Confirmed-good slice work is committed, pushed, and Hylla is refreshed to that commit.

child_rules.description_template: Have `qa-proof-agent` verify evidence completeness, trace coherence, claimed behavior support, Go naming and structure fit, error and logging discipline, testability, and justified simplicity for the parent dogfood refactor task.

Have `qa-falsification-agent` try to falsify the conclusion through counterexamples, alternate traces, hidden dependencies, contract mismatches, YAGNI pressure, and unnecessary abstraction checks for the parent dogfood refactor task.
Test the refactored slice against the dev version after both QA passes and confirm it still behaves like the pre-refactor baseline before closeout.
Confirm the locally used dogfood version was updated to the intended dev build before this slice is treated as complete.

Have `commit-and-reingest-agent` commit confirmed-good slice work, push the baseline that downstream tooling should rely on, trigger Hylla refresh, wait for the artifact to catch up to that commit, and record the resulting freshness evidence for downstream reasoning.

After QA and dev-version validation, update the task description with truthful slice metrics: git-diff added/removed/net lines, tracked-source line counts before and after, touched files/packages, Hylla node/block/orphan counts before and after, active/wait/ingest timing windows, ingest elapsed and cost when available, cleanup/security findings, the dev-version result, and final post-ingest totals; then hand the rollup to the orchestrator for the parent phase description and report artifact. Tillsyn generates this checkpoint, but it does not auto-verify every metric field or rollup total today, so record them explicitly and truthfully.
